cards.q: What is the title of Chapter 9?
cards.a: Schema Objects: Indexes, Views, Synonyms, and Sequences (الصفحة 2)

cards.q: Name the four schema objects covered in this chapter.
cards.a: Indexes, Views, Synonyms, and Sequences (الصفحة 2)

cards.q: According to the objectives listed, what is the first object type to be created?
cards.a: Create indexes (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: Name an object that can be created after Indexes and before Sequences.
cards.a: Views or Synonyms (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: What is the main benefit of creating an index?
cards.a: It can increase query performance by allowing the database to retrieve all data without having to retrieve all of a column's data. (الصفحة 5)

cards.q: Name two potential drawbacks or costs associated with indexes.
cards.a: They consume disk space and system resources, and they can cause a decrease in database performance and greater cost for DML statements. (الصفحة 5)

cards.q: When should DBAs/developers consider creating an index?
cards.a: Proactively (when first deploying an application) or Reactively (when application performance bogs down). (الصفحة 5)

cards.q: Describe the structure of a B-tree index.
cards.a: It consists of a root, branch blocks (which point to the next level), and leaf nodes (which contain entries that point to rows in the table). (الصفحة 5)

cards.q: What is an index in a database?
cards.a: An optionally created database object used primarily to increase query performance. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: Without indexes, what process occurs with each DML operation on the table?
cards.a: The entire table is scanned (full table scan). (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: Name two situations where DBAs and developers decide to create indexes.
cards.a: Proactively (when first deploying an application) or Reactively (when application performance bogs down). (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: What are multicolumn indexes also known as, and when are they effective?
cards.a: Concatenated indexes (or composite indexes); they are especially effective when multiple columns are used in the WHERE clause. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: Besides columns in the SELECT and WHERE clauses, what other SQL constructs might benefit from creating indexes?
cards.a: Columns used in the ORDER BY, GROUP BY, UNION, and DISTINCT clauses. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: What is a B-tree index, and for what type of columns is it generally recommended?
cards.a: It is the default index, good for columns with high cardinality (i.e., high degree of distinct values). (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: Describe the three levels found in the structure of a B-tree index.
cards.a: The root (contains entries pointing to the next level), branch blocks (point to the next level), and leaf nodes (contain index entries that point to rows). (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: What are the components of an index entry found in the leaf blocks?
cards.a: Entry header, Key column length-value pairs, and ROWID. (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: Under what conditions are B-Tree indexes recommended?
cards.a: When the cardinality in the column is high, the number of rows in the table is high, and the column is used in WHERE clauses or JOIN conditions (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: What type of environment are Bitmap indexes excellent for?
cards.a: Data warehouse environments with low cardinality columns and SQL statements using many AND or OR operators in the WHERE clause (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: How is a bitmap index structured differently from standard indices?
cards.a: The leaf node stores a bitmap for each key value instead of a list of row IDs. Each bit corresponds to a possible row ID, and if the bit is set, the row contains the key value (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: Why are Bitmap indexes not appropriate for OLTP databases?
cards.a: Because rows are frequently updated, and you cannot create a unique bitmap index (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: What components are contained in the leaf node of a bitmap index?
cards.a: An entry header, key values, Start ROWID, End ROWID, and a bitmap segment (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: When is it recommended to use Bitmap indexes?
cards.a: When the cardinality in the column is low, the number of rows in the table is high, and the column is used in Boolean algebra operations (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: What is the primary recommendation for managing space in a database regarding tables and indexes?
cards.a: It is recommended to separate tables and indexes into different tablespaces (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: Name three reasons for separating tables and indexes into different tablespaces.
cards.a: Doing so allows for differing backup and recovery requirements, accounts for different storage requirements, and can increase the performance of actions when on separate controllers (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: What can cause dramatic performance degradation when creating indexes?
cards.a: Creating indexes on the wrong columns or using features in the wrong situations (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: What type of index is discussed for creation?
cards.a: B-tree Indexes (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: To create an index in Toad of Oracle, what should the user follow?
cards.a: Follow steps provided in the video (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: When creating an index, besides the name and schema, what basic info must be entered?
cards.a: The table's name and type of index (الصفحة 20)

cards.q: What is the SQL statement used to create a B-tree index on an existing table?
cards.a: `CREATE INDEX cust_idx1 ON cust(last_name);` (الصفحة 24)

cards.q: How can you specify a tablespace when creating an index in Oracle?
cards.a: By using the syntax: `CREATE INDEX [index_name] ON [table_name]([column]) TABLESPACE [tablespace_name];` (الصفحة 24)

cards.q: Which view provides details about the structure of B-tree indexes in Oracle?
cards.a: The `INDEX_STATS DBA/ALL/USER_INDEXES` view. (الصفحة 24)

cards.q: What information does the INDEX_STATS view contain, and when is it populated?
cards.a: It contains information regarding HEIGHT (number of blocks from root to leaf blocks) and LF_ROWS (number of index entries), and it is only populated after you analyze the structure of the index. (الصفحة 24)

cards.q: What are concatenated indexes, also known as multicolumn indexes?
cards.a: Indexes that contain more than one column, which are especially effective when multiple columns are used in the WHERE clause when accessing a table. (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: How can you create a unique index using SQL?
cards.a: Use the UNIQUE clause, such as `create unique index cust_uk1 on cust(first_name, last_name);`. (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: When does Oracle automatically create a unique index?
cards.a: When you create a primary key constraint or a unique key constraint. (الصفحة 26)

cards.q: Which views should be looked at to view the details of a unique index that has been created by a primary key or unique key constraint?
cards.a: DBA_INDEXES and DBA_IND_COLUMNS. (الصفحة 27)

cards.q: What statement is used to rename an index in Oracle SQL?
cards.a: Use the ALTER INDEX ... RENAME TO statement (الصفحة 28)

cards.q: Which package can be used to display the DDL required to re-create an Index?
cards.a: DBMS_METADATA package (الصفحة 28)

cards.q: What is the advantage of marking an index UNUSABLE rather than dropping it?
cards.a: If later determined that the index is being used, you can alter it to a USABLE state and rebuild it without needing the DDL on hand (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: What statement is used to drop an index?
cards.a: Use the DROP INDEX statement (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: What are views?
cards.a: Representations of queries of data from one or more tables or other views. (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: Do views actually contain data?
cards.a: No; they derive their data from the base tables on which they are based. (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: What is one of the common uses for views regarding security and complexity?
cards.a: Views provide an additional level of security by restricting access to a predetermined set of rows and columns of a table, and they also hide data complexity. (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: When operations are performed on a view, what is actually affected?
cards.a: All operations performed on a view actually affect the base tables of the view. (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: ما هي العناصر الثلاثة التي يجب إدخالها لتنفيذ الاستعلام؟
cards.a: اسم المخطط (schema name)، واسم العرض (view name)، وعبارة SELECT. (الصفحة 34)

cards.q: ما هو الإجراء الذي يمثل الخطوة الأولى (step1) في العملية الموضحة؟
cards.a: إدخال اسم المخطط واسم العرض وعبارة SELECT. (الصفحة 34)

cards.q: ما هي العبارة المستخدمة لاسترداد البيانات عند تنفيذ الاستعلام؟
cards.a: عبارة SELECT. (الصفحة 34)

cards.q: ماذا يجب إدخاله قبل استخدام عبارة SELECT في سياق العمليات الموضحة؟
cards.a: اسم المخطط واسم العرض. (الصفحة 34)
